name: Sarma
recipeIngredient: 1 can Tomato soup (10 ½ oz size)
recipeInstructions:
1. Remove any bruised leaves from the cabbage and cut out the center core.
2. Pour boiling water over the cabbages to soften them.
3. In a separate pan, sauté bacon and onion until the bacon is cooked through.
4. Add half of the tomato soup to the pan with the bacon and onion mixture.
5. Beat eggs and add salt, pepper, paprika, Worcestershire sauce, ground beef, pork, and ham. Mix thoroughly.
6. Separate cabbage leaves and drain them.
7. Place a heaping tablespoon of stuffing at the core end of each cabbage leaf and roll carefully, tucking in the ends.
8. Arrange the rolls in layers in a Dutch oven or heavy kettle.
9. Chop any unused cabbage leaves and place them over the top.
10. Pour the remaining tomato soup and enough water into the pot to cover the rolls.
11. Cover and simmer for 2 to 2-1/2 hours.
